topHotspots.title: 中央财政下达育儿补贴补助资金999亿元
topHotspots.displayTitle: 中央财政下达育儿补贴补助资金999亿元
topHotspots.displaySummary: 记者6月2日从财政部了解到，为贯彻落实党中央、国务院决策部署，财政部近期下达2026年育儿补贴补助资金999亿元、较上年增长10.6%，支持各地为符合条件的婴幼儿发放育儿补贴，预计全年各级财政共安排补贴资金约1100亿元。

items.originalSummary: Learn how Googlers used AI to produce Google I/O 2026.
items.contentExcerpt: From the jellyfish pre-show to our “TPU Training Day” film, see how Gemini helped make I/O happen this year. Your browser does not support the audio element. Google I/O 2026 was all about how we’re making AI helpful for everyone in new ways. But we didn’t just make announcements about our innovations in AI at I/O — we used those tools to bring I/O to life, too. It’s both a strange and exciting moment to be building anything. We're living through an incredible shift where AI tools are getting...

items.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.displayTitle: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.originalTitle: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.summary: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.

items.title: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
items.displayTitle: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
items.originalTitle: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
items.summary: Scott Wray’s experience with spacewalks started when he was about 6 years old. A tent resembling a lunar lander provided the perfect imaginary spacecraft. “I would lie on my back with my feet propped up on a pillow as I imagined going through a launch countdown sequence,” he said. “Then I would exit the tent […]
items.displaySummary: Scott Wray’s experience with spacewalks started when he was about 6 years old. A tent resembling a lunar lander provided the perfect imaginary spacecraft. “I would lie on my bac...
items.originalSummary: Scott Wray’s experience with spacewalks started when he was about 6 years old. A tent resembling a lunar lander provided the perfect imaginary spacecraft. “I would lie on my back with my feet propped up on a pillow as I imagined going through a launch countdown sequence,” he said. “Then I would exit the tent […]
items.contentExcerpt: Scott Wray’s experience with spacewalks started when he was about 6 years old. A tent resembling a lunar lander provided the perfect imaginary spacecraft. “I would lie on my back with my feet propped up on a pillow as I imagined going through a launch countdown sequence,” he said. “Then I would exit the tent into a darkened bedroom and hop around just like the footage I had seen of Apollo astronauts.” Today, with more than 16 years at NASA’s Johnson Space Center under his belt, Wray is proud...

items.title: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
items.displayTitle: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
items.originalTitle: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
items.summary: NASA selected Denmar Technical Services of Nevada to provide aircraft modifications, maintenance, and testing services to the Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, and Johnson Space Center in Houston. The award is a firm-fixed-price contract and will be time and material for any over and above and unforeseen […]
items.displaySummary: NASA selected Denmar Technical Services of Nevada to provide aircraft modifications, maintenance, and testing services to the Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate at NASA’s Arm...
items.originalSummary: NASA selected Denmar Technical Services of Nevada to provide aircraft modifications, maintenance, and testing services to the Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, and Johnson Space Center in Houston. The award is a firm-fixed-price contract and will be time and material for any over and above and unforeseen […]
items.contentExcerpt: NASA selected Denmar Technical Services of Nevada to provide aircraft modifications, maintenance, and testing services to the Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, and Johnson Space Center in Houston. The award is a firm-fixed-price contract and will be time and material for any over and above and unforeseen work. This contract has a maximum potential value of $8.4 million, which runs through Feb. 1, 2027. The contractor will...

items.title: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.displayTitle: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.originalTitle: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.summary: Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area.  Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help […]
items.displaySummary: Johnson Space Center Office of Communications Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights a...
items.originalSummary: Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area.  Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help […]
items.contentExcerpt: Johnson Space Center Office of Communications Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area. Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help researchers map the movement of the gases...

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.displayTitle: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.originalTitle: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.summary: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.
items.displaySummary: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes...
items.originalSummary: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.

Follow live here from New York.

The award is a firm-fixed-price contract and will be time and material for any over and above and unforeseen […]

items.contentExcerpt: NASA selected Denmar Technical Services of Nevada to provide aircraft modifications, maintenance, and testing services to the Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, and Johnson Space Center in Houston. The award is a firm-fixed-price contract and will be time and material for any over and above and unforeseen work. This contract has a maximum potential value of $8.4 million, which runs through Feb. 1, 2027. The contractor will...

items.title: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.displayTitle: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.originalTitle: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
items.summary: Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area.  Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help […]

Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area.  Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help […]
items.contentExcerpt: Johnson Space Center Office of Communications Five research aircraft will support a Student Airborne Research Program (SARP) mission out of Ellington Field in Houston. Flights are expected from Wednesday, June 3 to Saturday, June 13. During the mission, select maneuvers will be conducted at low altitudes over the Houston area. Pilots will fly remote sensing payloads in raster patterns, or parallel back-and-forth lines. The instruments flown could help researchers map the movement of the gases...

items.contentExcerpt: From the jellyfish pre-show to our “TPU Training Day” film, see how Gemini helped make I/O happen this year. Your browser does not support the audio element. Google I/O 2026 was all about how we’re making AI helpful for everyone in new ways. But we didn’t just make announcements about our innovations in AI at I/O — we used those tools to bring I/O to life, too. It’s both a strange and exciting moment to be building anything. We're living through an incredible shift where AI tools are getting...

items.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.displayTitle: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.originalTitle: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.summary: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.displayTitle: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.originalTitle: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
items.summary: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.

items.title: Fire’s Footprint on Santa Rosa Island

items.originalTitle: Fire’s Footprint on Santa Rosa Island
items.summary: A wildland fire charred grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of the island, the second largest of the Channel Islands.
items.displaySummary: A wildland fire charred grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of the island, the second largest of the Channel Islands. On May 15, 2026, a fire was spott...
items.originalSummary: A wildland fire charred grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of the island, the second largest of the Channel Islands.
items.contentExcerpt: A wildland fire charred grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of the island, the second largest of the Channel Islands. On May 15, 2026, a fire was spotted from aircraft on the southeastern side of Santa Rosa Island, part of California’s Channel Islands National Park. The blaze spread over the next several days, ultimately burning 18,379 acres (7,438 hectares)—about one-third of the island. These images show the expansion of the fire’s burned area between May 16 (left)...

items.originalTitle: What’s Up: June 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
items.summary: Venus and Jupiter meet after sunset, the Moon passes in front of Venus, summer begins, and deep-sky treasures rise into view.

items.originalSummary: Venus and Jupiter meet after sunset, the Moon passes in front of Venus, summer begins, and deep-sky treasures rise into view.
items.contentExcerpt: Venus and Jupiter meet after sunset, the Moon passes in front of Venus, summer begins, and deep-sky treasures rise into view. Planets gather after sunset, the Moon passes in front of Venus, summer officially begins and deep sky treasures rise into view. That's What's Up for June. Early this month, look west shortly after sunset to see Venus and Jupiter. They are two of the brightest planets in our sky and around June 9th, they'll appear close together after sunset. This is called a planetary...

Scores of gleaming stars ringed in neon pink stretch across the frame, highlighting a cluster where stars are between one and three million years […]

items.contentExcerpt: This image of Westerlund 2 released on March 19, 2026, features Chandra X-ray Observatory data (pink) and infrared data from NASA’S James Webb Space Telescope (red, orange, green, cyan, and blue). Scores of gleaming stars ringed in neon pink stretch across the frame, highlighting a cluster where stars are between one and three million years old. Brick-orange dust clouds along the bottom edge illustrate the raw materials of this active stellar nursery. Westerlund 2 resides in a raucous stellar...

items.title: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
items.displayTitle: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
items.originalTitle: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
items.summary: Years after conflicts fade from the headlines, the weapons used to fight them often continue to circulate – crossing borders, fuelling crime and undermining an often-fragile peace. Now, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms and increasingly sophisticated trafficking networks are creating new challenges for governments worldwide.
